qa_pairs.question: How does one compile a minimal LibTorch C++ example project using `cmake` when working within the `StdEnv/2023` environment?
qa_pairs.answer: To compile a minimal LibTorch C++ example project with `StdEnv/2023`, you first configure it using the command: `cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=$VIRTUAL_ENV/lib/python3.11/site-packages -DCMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS="-Wl,-rpath=$VIRTUAL_ENV/lib/python3.11/site-packages/torch/lib,-L$EBROOTCUDA/extras/CUPTI/lib64" -DCMAKE_SKIP_RPATH=ON -DTORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="6.0;7.0;7.5;8.0;9.0"`. After configuration, build the program by executing `cmake --build build`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the `cmake` commands required to compile a minimal LibTorch C++ example project in the `StdEnv/2020` environment?
qa_pairs.answer: For the `StdEnv/2020` environment, you configure the project with the command: `cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=$VIRTUAL_ENV/lib/python3.10/site-packages -DCMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS="-Wl,-rpath=$VIRTUAL_ENV/lib/python3.10/site-packages/torch/lib" -DCMAKE_SKIP_RPATH=ON`. Following configuration, compile the program by running `cmake --build build`.
